description: Bucket-organized watchlists for Aggressive Momentum + Event Switch strategy. Each bucket maps to a regime preference. Read by shared/regime.py + entry monitors via shared/profile.load_watchlists().

ai_nasdaq_semis._comment: Mega-cap + semis leaders. RISK_ON: full size. NEUTRAL: 70% size. Avoid in RISK_OFF / INFLATION_SHOCK.

inflation_energy._comment: Oil + energy + sector ETF. Trigger when CPI surprise / OPEC+ cut / geopolitical oil shock. XOM already used by geo-monitor in geo-defense bucket — that integration kept; this bucket is the price-momentum entry path.

crypto._comment: Crypto sizing is per-tier (Tier 1 BTC/ETH vs Tier 2 alts). Already configured in crypto-monitor/monitor.py::COIN_TIERS — this list is purely the universe for regime allowlist. size_per_position_usd=0 means 'see COIN_TIERS'.

hedge_metals._comment: Gold/silver hedge. Smaller stop because metals less volatile. Used in INFLATION_SHOCK (inflation hedge), RISK_OFF (flight to safety).

hedge_bonds._comment: 20+ year Treasury ETF. Rallies when fear spikes + Fed pivots dovish. Use only in RISK_OFF.

leveraged_etf_bull._comment: 3× bull ETFs. Path-dependent (decay in chop). Use ONLY when clear directional regime confirmed by inferred regime + momentum score. NEVER in NEUTRAL.

leveraged_etf_bear._comment: 3× bear ETFs. Tactical short-side when SPY breakdown confirmed. NEVER hold through weekend (path decay). Time-limited 24-48h.

defense_geo._comment: Defense bucket, used by defense-monitor + geo-monitor. Surfaces in INFLATION_SHOCK (geo escalation correlates with oil shocks) and RISK_OFF (flight to government-revenue safety).

software_quality._comment: Software/cloud quality compounders. Added 2026-05-21 v3.9.1 — Senior PM agent flagged NOW (ServiceNow) as diversification opportunity vs 80% ai_nasdaq_semis concentration. Lower beta than semis (1.0-1.2), recurring revenue, less gap risk. Use in RISK_ON (alongside semis) and NEUTRAL (preferred over semis when score similar). Avoid in RISK_OFF / INFLATION_SHOCK — high-multiple growth names sell off on rate fears.
